name: Zebras
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 13x9-inch baking pan and line with foil. Grease the foil.
2. Melt 1 cup chocolate chips in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring often. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
3. Mix flour and baking powder. In a large bowl, beat butter and sugar with an electric mixer until fluffy. Beat in eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Add vanilla extract. Stir in the flour mixture until blended. Transfer half of this batter to a separate bowl.
4. Stir melted chocolate into the remaining batter until fully incorporated. Spread the chocolate batter evenly in the prepared pan. Drop spoonfuls of the plain batter over the chocolate layer and spread carefully to form an even layer. Sprinkle with the remaining 1/2 cup chocolate chips and walnuts.
5. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes until the edges pull away from the sides of the pan.
6. Cool in the pan on a wire rack before cutting into bars. Makes 24 bars. Per bar: 221 calories, 3 grams protein, 25 grams carbohydrates, 13 grams fat, 38 milligrams cholesterol with butter, 18 milligrams cholesterol with margarine, 108 milligrams sodium. Exchanges: 1 starch/bread, 2/3 fruit, 2.5 fat. Source: February 1994 Woman's Day Magazine
